name: Washington Dietitian Certification
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: Washington State Department of Health, PO Box 47877, Olympia, WA 98504-7877

renewalCycle: Annual (on birthday) + No state CE requirement

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from the educational institution to the Washington DOH.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: HIV/AIDS Training Certificate
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of at least 4 hours of approved HIV/AIDS training.

requiredDocuments.name: Credential Verification from Other States
requiredDocuments.description: If you hold credentials in other states, verification must be sent directly from each state's licensing authority.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Washington dietitian certification cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $81 ($65 application + $16 mandatory HEAL-WA surcharge). Annual renewal is $61 ($45 renewal + $16 HEAL-WA surcharge).

mainEntity.name: Are there continuing education requirements for Washington dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Washington State does not require continuing education for state certification renewal. However, to maintain the CDR registration that is a prerequisite for certification, you must complete 75 continuing professional education units every five years through the CDR.

mainEntity.name: When does the Washington dietitian certification need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Certification must be renewed annually on or before your birthday. As of May 2025, the DOH no longer sends printed renewal notices; all notifications are electronic through the HELMS portal.

mainEntity.name: Does Washington have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Washington has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Washington?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
